purpose: A complete, standalone coaching system for the fixed 8-run / 8-station fitness race format: athlete assessment, deterministic goal-time modeling, periodized training plans, running and compromised-running development, station technique and conditioning, race pacing and roxzone transitions, simulations, race-week taper, division and partner strategy, and post-race analysis. Domain-general coaching concepts are specialized for this race format, not factored out as generic fitness advice.
intended_users: First-time and recreational athletes preparing for their first race
Competitive single-division athletes chasing a goal time
Doubles, mixed-doubles, and relay teams planning partner strategy
Coaches who want structured, assumption-explicit programming and analysis
Returning athletes rebuilding after a layoff or a disappointing result
routing_guide: Start at hyrox-pack-router for any broad or multi-part request; it routes to the narrowest applicable skill(s). Direct entry points: assessment -> hyrox-athlete-assessment; 'what splits to break X' -> hyrox-goal-time-and-race-predictor then hyrox-race-pacing-planner; 'N weeks to race' -> hyrox-training-plan-builder; falling apart after a station -> hyrox-compromised-running-coach; review my result -> hyrox-post-race-performance-analyzer; should I race after illness -> hyrox-race-readiness-decision-tool. Standalone running development (hyrox-running-development-plan) is distinct from compromised running (hyrox-compromised-running-coach); goal-time prediction (predictor) is distinct from in-race pacing (pacing-planner); station technique (technique-and-fault-analyzer) is distinct from station conditioning (station-progression-builder); race readiness (readiness-decision-tool) is coaching judgment, never medical clearance.
example_workflows: Assessment -> goal-time prediction -> training plan -> simulation -> race pacing -> taper -> post-race analysis
Race result review -> post-race analyzer -> adaptive plan manager updates the next block
Doubles team intake -> athlete assessment (x2) -> doubles strategy builder -> simulation builder (doubles) -> race pacing
Sick this week -> race-readiness decision tool -> (if racing) revised pacing plan + taper adjustment
safety_behavior: Every coaching skill applies the shared safety-and-referral framework proportionately: it does not diagnose, provide medical or physiotherapy rehab, override clinician instructions, prescribe medication, treat eating disorders, promise injury prevention, or guarantee race outcomes. It flags professional evaluation for red-flag symptoms (chest pain, syncope, severe shortness of breath, neurological symptoms, suspected fracture, major trauma, acute joint instability, rapidly increasing swelling, severe or unexplained pain, rhabdomyolysis or heat-illness warning signs, disordered-eating indicators, pregnancy/postpartum complications, and symptoms worsening or not responding as expected).
rule_version_status: Division weights, standards, and seasonal rules are never hardcoded. The rules-dependent skills (hyrox-division-and-rules-advisor, hyrox-doubles-strategy-builder, hyrox-relay-strategy-builder) require a season or event date, use only the matching version from references/source-version-registry.json, warn when a rule may be stale, request current rule text when it is missing, and distinguish official rule vs interpretation vs common practice vs unverified assumption. The registry ships empty of proprietary rulebooks by default.
notes: This independent skill repository is not official, endorsed, sponsored, approved, or certified by HYROX World GmbH or its affiliates. "HYROX" is a trademark of HYROX World GmbH; references here are nominative only. Absent documented brand authorization, the neutral display name "Fitness Racing Performance Pack" is used.
